View this page

Limitation de débit¶

La limitation de débit contrôle le nombre de requêtes qu’un client peut envoyer à une route dans une fenêtre de temps définie. Nexus Gateway applique ces limites au niveau de la passerelle, avant que les requêtes n’atteignent les services backend.

Pourquoi la limitation de débit est importante¶

Without rate limiting, a single misbehaving client — whether due to a bug, misconfiguration, or deliberate abuse — can consume enough resources to degrade service for all other clients.

La limitation de débit protège votre infrastructure en :

  • Empêchant les clients individuels de saturer les services backend.

  • Réduisant l’impact des attaques par déni de service.

  • Appliquant des quotas d’utilisation pour les clients sur différents niveaux tarifaires.

  • Lissant les pics de trafic causés par des traitements par lot ou des tempêtes de relance.

Comment Nexus Gateway compte les requêtes¶

Nexus Gateway uses a sliding window algorithm by default. The window moves continuously rather than resetting at fixed intervals, which prevents bursts of traffic at window boundaries.

A fixed window option is also available for use cases where predictable reset times are required, such as billing-cycle quota enforcement.

Identifiants de limite de débit¶

Rate limits are applied per identifier. The identifier determines how the gateway groups requests for counting purposes. Supported identifiers are:

  • IP address — limits requests from a single IP. Useful as a baseline protection layer.

  • API key — limits requests per authenticated client. The most common approach for API products.

  • JWT claim — limits requests based on a value from the token payload, such as a sub or org_id claim.

  • Header value — limits requests based on any request header. Useful for custom client identification schemes.

Identifiers can be combined. For example, you can apply both a per-IP limit and a per-API-key limit to the same route. Both limits are enforced independently.

En-têtes de réponse de limite de débit¶

When rate limiting is active on a route, Nexus Gateway includes standard rate limit headers in every response so clients can track their usage:

Header

Description

X-RateLimit-Limit

The maximum number of requests allowed in the window.

X-RateLimit-Remaining

The number of requests remaining in the current window.

X-RateLimit-Reset

The Unix timestamp when the window resets.

Retry-After

Seconds to wait before retrying. Present only on 429 responses.

When a client exceeds the limit, the gateway returns a 429 Too Many Requests response and stops forwarding the request to the upstream service.

Configuring Rate Limits¶

Rate limits are defined in the route configuration. The following example applies a limit of 100 requests per minute per API key:

routes:
  - path: /api/v1/products
    upstream: products-service:8080
    rate_limit:
      requests: 100
      window: 60s
      identifier: api_key

Astuce

Start with generous limits and tighten them based on observed traffic patterns. Setting limits too low before understanding your clients” request patterns leads to unnecessary friction.

Nexus Gateway Version 2.4
